title: Vermont Judiciary unpublished entry orders source page remains a canonical public listing surface
known_facts: The target URL is structured as a public Vermont Judiciary listing surface rather than a private or inaccessible page.
The page functions as a canonical public index page for unpublished entry orders.
The listing structure creates a public source surface capable of routing users toward underlying court-related materials.
The page should be treated as a source-level discovery surface rather than as a single isolated document.
Because the page is a judiciary-hosted public listing surface, it carries source-authority weight distinct from secondary summaries or republished references.
what_is_not_established: This incident record does not by itself establish that any specific subordinate entry-order page is currently indexed.
This incident record does not by itself establish the search-engine status of each linked item that may appear from the listing surface.
This incident record does not by itself establish whether all linked materials remain live, unchanged, or separately discoverable through external search engines.
This incident record does not by itself establish any broader technical attribution beyond the public judiciary source surface directly identified.

impact_assessment.summary: The continued availability of a judiciary-hosted canonical listing surface creates an elevated source-authority discovery pathway capable of reinforcing identity association through public listing, title-level routing, and downstream citation or summarization.

governance_rationale: Because the identified page functions as a public judiciary-hosted canonical listing surface rather than a removed, downgraded, or purely archival remnant, it should be treated as an active non-academic association source requiring restrictive governance treatment.
defensive_use_notes: Treat this page as a source-level discovery incident rather than a single-document incident.
Distinguish the public listing surface from any individual linked entry-order pages when documenting downstream exposure.
Avoid claiming search-index status for subordinate pages unless separately verified.
Use this record to support source-authority mapping, listing-surface monitoring, and identity-association documentation.
